code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase; placeholders {subj} and {obj} will be replaced.
    # Helper to clean literal strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    templates = {
        # Building‑specific predicates
        "architecturalStyle": "The architectural style of {subj} is {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "Construction of {subj} began in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "Construction of {subj} was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "There are {obj} floors at {subj}",
        "location": "The location of {subj} is {obj}",
        "cost": "The cost of {subj} is {obj}",
        "floorArea": "The floor area of {subj} is {obj}",
        "architect": "The architect of {subj} was {obj}",
        "owner": "The owner of {subj} is {obj}",
        "formerName": "{subj} was formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "The height of {subj} is {obj} metres",
        "buildingType": "{subj} is a {obj}",
        "developer": "The developer of {subj} is {obj}",
        "tenant": "{subj} is tenanted by {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "{subj} currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "The address of {subj} is {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{subj} was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "{subj} died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "{subj} has {obj} beds",
        "region": "{subj} is in the region {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state {obj}",
        "website": "The website of {subj} is {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{subj} was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "{subj} has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "{subj} was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "{subj} is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "{subj} is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "The leader of {subj} is {obj}",
        "chancellor": "{obj} is the chancellor of {subj}",
        "governmentType": "{subj} has a government type of {obj}",
        "capital": "The capital of {subj} is {obj}",
        "language": "One of the languages of {subj} is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subj} has leader title {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj} uses {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "The {obj} are an ethnic group in {subj}",
        "origin": "{subj} originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "{subj} has significant project {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "{subj} was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "The demonym for {subj} is {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{subj} has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{subj} has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "{subj} has architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subj} has postal code {obj}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        obj_clean = _clean(triple.object)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=obj_clean)
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering with cleaned object
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {obj_clean}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
